| Component | Description | Docs |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Dialog | Modal dialog with header, content, footer | [overlay.md](docs/overlay.md) |
| Sheet | Slide-out panel (top, right, bottom, left) | [overlay.md](docs/overlay.md) |
| DropdownMenu | Context/action menu | [overlay.md](docs/overlay.md) |
| Tooltip | Hover tooltip | [overlay.md](docs/overlay.md) |
| Popover | Click-triggered popover | [overlay.md](docs/overlay.md) |
| Command | Command palette with search | [overlay.md](docs/overlay.md) |

### Navigation

| Component | Description | Docs |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Tabs | Tab panels (pill and line variants) | [navigation.md](docs/navigation.md) |
| Accordion | Collapsible sections | [navigation.md](docs/navigation.md) |
| Collapsible | Single collapsible section | [navigation.md](docs/navigation.md) |
| Pagination | Page navigation | [navigation.md](docs/navigation.md) |
| ScrollArea | Custom scrollbar container | [navigation.md](docs/navigation.md) |
| Toggle | Single toggle button | [navigation.md](docs/navigation.md) |
| ToggleGroup | Group of toggle buttons | [navigation.md](docs/navigation.md) |

## Decision Guide: Which Component to Use

For **variant / tone / surface / size choices** within a chosen component (e.g. which Badge tone? Modal vs Dialog vs Sheet? `default` vs `sm` Button? human тЖТ circle vs entity тЖТ square Avatar?), see [decision-guide.md](docs/decision-guide.md). The table below picks the *component*; the linked doc picks the *variant*.

| Scenario | Component |
|----------|-----------|
| App-wide layout with sidebar nav | AppShell |
| Page title + description + actions | PageHeader |
| Detail/show page for a resource | DetailPage |
| Form modal with sticky header/footer | Modal |
| Short static dropdown (< 10 items) | Select |
| Searchable dropdown, long lists | SelectPicker |
| Multiple selection from a list | SelectPicker (mode="multiple") |
| Pick a single date | DatePicker |
| Pick a date range | DateRangePicker |
| File upload with drag & drop | AttachmentInput (variant="dropzone") |
| Inline file upload button | AttachmentInput (variant="compact") |
| Card-based single/multi selection | SelectableCard |
| Standard action (create/edit/delete) | Button (action prop) |
| Primary + secondary actions | SplitButton |
| Search/filter input with debounce | SearchInput |
| Bounded numeric input (score, percent, hours, weights) | NumberInput |
| Password input with show/hide toggle | PasswordInput |
| Table filtering with chips | FilterButton + ActiveFilters |
| Multi-step wizard progress | Stepper |
| Animated score/metric display | AnimatedNumber |
| Card with one progress + breakdown counts | ProgressStatCard |
| Collapsible long text | ExpandableText |
| Confirmation before destructive action | AlertDialog |
| Form modal / side panel | Dialog or Sheet |
| Inline monospace ID with copy | CodeLabel |
| Toast notifications | Sonner (toast) |
| Status indicator | Badge |
| Loading placeholder | Skeleton |
| Full-screen loading state | LoadingPage |
| Offline/connectivity warning | ConnectionBanner |
| Error boundary fallback | ErrorFallback |

## Styling Conventions

- Always support dark mode via the `.dark` class (set on `<html>` or an ancestor) plus `dark:` Tailwind variants
- All theme colors are CSS variables defined in `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui/styles.css` (stone neutrals + panda-blue accent + semantic success/info/warning/danger)
- Color space: OKLCH for `bg-pattern-*` utilities; design tokens use HEX for portability
- Icons: Lucide React (`lucide-react`)
- Fonts: Inter (sans), Comfortaa (display), JetBrains Mono (mono) тАФ load via Google Fonts in your `index.html`

### Heading font policy (strict)

Comfortaa (`var(--font-display)`) is reserved for **two surfaces only**:

1. The `<PageHeader>` component title (already wired internally тАФ do not re-apply).
2. The `text-display-sm/md/lg/xl` utilities тАФ large hero/marketing text.

**Everything else in the app renders in Inter (`var(--font-sans)`)** тАФ including all card titles, dialog/modal titles, sheet titles, sidebar section labels, table headers, accordion triggers, page subheadings, `<h2>`/`<h3>`/`<h4>` in user-authored content, etc.

Rules for AI agents and humans editing consumer projects:

- **DO NOT add a global `h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 { font-family: var(--font-display) }` rule.** Lucid UI's stylesheet now ships an explicit reset that pins headings to Inter; a downstream global heading rule defeats the policy and re-introduces the brand face across the entire app. If you find such a rule in a consumer's `globals.css` / `index.css` / `app.css`, remove it.
- **DO NOT** wrap `<CardTitle>`, `<DialogTitle>`, `<SheetTitle>`, `<AccordionTrigger>`, etc. in `font-display` to "match the page header" тАФ those surfaces are deliberately Inter.
- **DO** add `className="font-display"` (or use a `text-display-*` utility) on a single element when you genuinely need Comfortaa on a marketing/hero surface inside a consumer app. Per-element opt-in only.
- **DO** keep the consumer's CSS import order as documented (`@import "tailwindcss"` тЖТ `@import "@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui/styles.css"` тЖТ consumer styles). Lucid UI's reset must come before consumer overrides for the policy to hold.

## Changelog

Machine-readable changelog for AI agents consuming this library.
When upgrading `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui` in a downstream project, use this section to determine what changed and what actions are required.

Each entry contains:
- **component**: the component name
- **change**: what changed (added | modified | removed | renamed)
- **action**: what consuming projects must do
- **breaking**: whether this is a breaking change
- **details**: concise description of the change

---

### 2026-05-04

#### foundations тАФ Colors / Typography / Tones showcase pages + llms.txt section

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: |
    Added a new "Foundations" sidebar group to the demo with three pages:
      - `/#/colors` тАФ stone scale, panda blue scale, semantic chroma, WCAG AA pairings (success/info/warning/destructive soft-fg + destructive-aa), adaptive theme tokens (background, foreground, card, primary, secondary, muted, accent, brand, destructive, border, input, input-bg, ring), and chart tokens. Each swatch shows the token name plus a click-to-copy hex/value caption.
      - `/#/typography` тАФ the three font families (Inter / Comfortaa / JetBrains Mono) with sample text, the body / display / mono size scales (size, line-height, weight, letter-spacing), and a "Heading font policy (strict)" callout with correct/incorrect examples.
      - `/#/tones` тАФ shared semantic-tone palette table (success / warning / info / destructive / muted / default) with paired Badge + MeterRow rendering, plus live demos of how `Badge`, `MeterRow`, `ProgressStatCard`, and `StatCard` `deltaTone` consume the tones.

    No new tokens, components, or utilities introduced тАФ these pages document tokens that already shipped. New showcase files: `apps/demo/src/showcase/demos/colors-demo.tsx`, `typography-demo.tsx`, `tones-demo.tsx`. The showcase app's `CATEGORIES` array gains a leading "Foundations" group so they appear above the component catalog in the sidebar.

    `public/llms.txt` gains a new "Foundations" section between the Tech Stack table and the Component Catalog, listing every color token (with hex), the three font families with their utilities, both size scales, and the tone vocabulary cross-referenced to the components that consume it. The decision-guide reference is preserved as the canonical "which Badge variant?"-style lookup.
- action:
  - No consumer action required тАФ these are documentation surfaces.
  - When choosing a color: reach for the adaptive theme tokens first (`bg-background`, `bg-card`, `bg-muted`, `bg-primary`, `bg-brand`); reach for raw stone/panda/chroma steps only when you genuinely need a specific neutral or non-text fill.
  - When pairing text on a tinted-bg control (badge, banner, chip), use `text-success-soft-fg` / `text-info-soft-fg` / `text-warning-soft-fg` / `text-destructive-soft-fg` rather than the chroma `text-success` / etc. тАФ only the soft-fg tokens clear AA on `bg-X/15` (light) and `bg-X/20` (dark).
  - When picking a tone for a Badge/MeterRow/ProgressStatCard cell/StatCard delta, pick by meaning (success = healthy, warning = needs attention, etc.) and match the same tone across paired components in a single chart.

---

#### password-input тАФ new component

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: |
    New `PasswordInput` thin wrapper around `Input` that renders an Eye / EyeOff icon button inside the field (right side, `pr-9` reserved on the input). Clicking the toggle flips the input's `type` between `password` and `text`. Forwards refs to the underlying input via `forwardRef` so it composes with `react-hook-form` register and other ref-based form libs. Accepts every standard `<input>` prop except `type` (owned by the toggle), plus `defaultVisible?: boolean` for one-time / temporary-password fields and `toggleLabel?: { show: string; hide: string }` for localized aria-labels.

    The toggle button sits in the natural tab order (no `tabIndex={-1}`), uses `aria-pressed` semantics, has a stateful `aria-label` ("Show password" / "Hide password"), gets `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring`, and disables in lockstep with the input's `disabled` prop.

    Visually it reuses the existing `--input-bg` form surface and the standard `focus-visible:ring-ring` focus treatment тАФ no new design tokens introduced. Icon comes from `lucide-react` (already a peer dep), so no new dependency added.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui` to pick up `PasswordInput`.
  - Replace any local "Input + manual eye-toggle button" wrapper with `<PasswordInput value={value} onChange={...} autoComplete="current-password" />`. For new-password screens, pass `autoComplete="new-password"`.
  - For temporary-password / OTP-style reveal-by-default fields, pass `defaultVisible`.

---

#### number-input тАФ new component

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: |
    New `NumberInput` controlled component that wraps `Input` + `Label`. Models the empty state as `value: number | ''` (matching what `<input value>` accepts) so callers don't have to translate `undefined` тЖФ `''`. Supports `minimum` / `maximum` inclusive bounds with clamping, `step` (defaults to `1`) which also drives `toFixed(decimalPlaces)` formatting on blur, an inline `suffix` rendered inside the field (`%`, `h`, `x`, ...) with the input padded to `pr-12`, тЖС/тЖУ keyboard stepping that clamps to bounds, and a non-passive `wheel` listener that prevents accidental value changes when the page scrolls with a focused input.

    Two messaging surfaces below the field: a transient info message (`text-info`, ~3s auto-dismiss) appears when blur clamps the typed value to `minimum`/`maximum`, and a persistent destructive message appears when the parent passes `error`. The persistent error suppresses the transient/helper text. The error state also flips the input to `aria-invalid` plus a `border-destructive` + `focus-visible:ring-destructive/40` outline.

    Imported from the Pandahrms-Performance project's local `src/components/ui/number-input.tsx` so multiple consumer apps can share the same constraint behavior. The validation helper lives in a sibling `number-input-validator.ts` so it can be unit-tested in isolation; it is not re-exported from the package barrel.

    Visually it reuses the existing `--input-bg` form surface and the `aria-invalid` / `border-destructive` error treatment, so it inherits the same dark-mode tokens as the rest of the form-fields family тАФ no new design tokens introduced.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui` to pick up `NumberInput`.
  - Replace any local hand-rolled "Input + decimal step + clamp + suffix + error" wrapper with `<NumberInput value={value} onChange={setValue} minimum={n} maximum={n} step={n} suffix="..." error={error} />`. If the consumer was using `value: number | undefined`, switch to `number | ''` (callers should map `undefined` тЖТ `''` before passing in).

---

### 2026-05-03

#### form-fields тАФ fields and outline buttons now render on a tinted `--input-bg` surface

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: |
    Added a new `--input-bg` design token (light: `var(--stone-100)` `#EFF3F6`, dark: `var(--stone-800)` `#1C2229`) and switched the field surface on `Input`, `Textarea`, `SelectTrigger`, outline `Button`, and `AttachmentInput`'s compact trigger from `bg-background` to `bg-input-bg`. Pickers (`DatePicker`, `DateRangePicker`, `SelectPicker`) inherit through `Button` outline; `SearchInput` inherits through `Input`.

    Previously every form surface used `bg-background` (`stone-50` `#F7F9FB` light / `stone-800` dark). In light mode that sat at ~1.02:1 against the `#FFFFFF` `Card`/`Dialog` surface, so inputs and outline buttons inside panels effectively blended in and only the 1px border carried the boundary. The new tint sits at a visible offset against both white cards and the page bg, while still reading as a "field" rather than a heavy filled control.

    Dark mode is intentionally unchanged in appearance тАФ `--input-bg: var(--stone-800)` resolves to the same value `bg-background` was already producing in dark mode, so the existing pressed-in-field look is preserved (input darker than the stone-700 card surface).

    Contrast: foreground text (`--foreground` stone-800) on `--input-bg` (stone-100) тЙИ 14.4:1 (AAA) light; placeholder (`--muted-foreground` stone-600) on `--input-bg` тЙИ 7.0:1 (AAA) light. Dark-mode contrast is unchanged from prior baseline.

    The `--input` token (input border color, `stone-200` light / `stone-600` dark) is unchanged тАФ this change adds a separate background token rather than repurposing the existing border token. The `@theme inline` block now exposes `--color-input-bg` so the `bg-input-bg` Tailwind utility resolves correctly.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui` to pick up the change.
  - If your consumer overrides `bg-background` on inputs locally (e.g. `Input className="bg-white"`), decide whether to drop that override (and inherit the new token) or keep it (and stay flat-on-card).
  - No code or markup changes required for default usage.

---

#### accordion тАФ animation keyframes + theme tokens shipped (silent no-op fix)

- change: fixed
- breaking: no
- details: |
    `AccordionContent` already applied `data-[state=closed]:animate-accordion-up data-[state=open]:animate-accordion-down` (driven by Radix's `data-state`), but the package never shipped the matching `@keyframes accordion-down` / `@keyframes accordion-up` or the `--animate-accordion-down` / `--animate-accordion-up` `@theme inline` tokens. The animation classes resolved to nothing, so AccordionContent collapsed/expanded instantly with no transition unless the consumer hand-defined the keyframes (against `--radix-accordion-content-height`) in their own `globals.css`.

    Added both keyframes and both animation tokens to [packages/registry/src/styles.css](packages/registry/src/styles.css). Animation now works out of the box.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui` to pick up the keyframes.
  - If your consumer's `globals.css` (or `index.css`) defines `@keyframes accordion-down` / `@keyframes accordion-up` and/or `--animate-accordion-down` / `--animate-accordion-up`, **delete those local copies** тАФ they are now duplicated in the package. Cascade order makes the local copy win silently, but it's redundant and will drift.

---

#### styles тАФ token-driven scrollbar styling shipped in `@layer base`

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: |
    Added Firefox (`scrollbar-width: thin`, `scrollbar-color: var(--stone-300) transparent`, dark-mode `var(--stone-600)`) and WebKit (`::-webkit-scrollbar` 10├Ч10, thumb on `--stone-300`/`--stone-400` light and `--stone-600`/`--stone-500` dark, transparent track, 6px radius) scrollbar rules to the package's base layer. The block uses only existing `--stone-*` tokens, so it tracks the design system's neutral scale automatically.

    Previously every consumer that wanted consistent scrollbar chrome rebuilt these rules locally against the same tokens (Pandahrms-Performance, etc.). Consolidating upstream removes that duplication and prevents drift if the stone scale ever shifts.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui`.
  - Delete any local `* { scrollbar-width: thin; scrollbar-color: var(--stone-300) transparent }` / `::-webkit-scrollbar*` block from your consumer's `globals.css` тАФ the package now provides it. Consumers with project-specific scrollbar overrides continue to win via cascade order; the package only fills the default.

---

#### typography тАФ Comfortaa scoped to PageHeader + display utilities; everything else reverts to Inter

- change: modified
- breaking: no (token names unchanged; visual change only)
- details: |
    Removed the global base-layer rule that applied Comfortaa to every `<h1>`тАУ`<h6>`, and replaced it with an explicit `h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 { font-family: var(--font-sans) }` reset inside `@layer base`. Comfortaa now renders only on the `PageHeader` title and the `text-display-sm/md/lg/xl` hero/marketing utilities; all other headings, body text, labels, and dialog/card titles render in Inter via `--font-sans`.

    The `--font-display` CSS variable and the `font-display` Tailwind utility are unchanged тАФ both still resolve to Comfortaa. `PageHeader` now applies `font-display` to its title `<h1>` so the brand face stays on that one component without leaking via global selectors. The `text-display-*` utilities continue to use `var(--font-display)`.

    A new "Heading font policy (strict)" section in this file (and a matching block in the project `CLAUDE.md`) documents the rule for AI agents and devs working in downstream consumers: do not add a global `h1тАУh6 { font-family: var(--font-display) }` rule, and remove any stale copy you find in a consumer's `globals.css` / `index.css` / `app.css`.

    No accessibility regression тАФ Inter is already the body face and clears AA at all sizes used in headings; Comfortaa retention on hero text is unchanged.
- action:
  - Audit consumer projects for a stray `h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 { font-family: var(--font-display) }` block (commonly in `globals.css` or `index.css`). If found, **delete it** тАФ it overrides Lucid UI's reset and re-introduces Comfortaa across the entire app.
  - If your app legitimately needs Comfortaa on a specific heading or hero element, add `className="font-display"` (or use a `text-display-*` utility) on that single element. Per-element opt-in only.
  - Confirm the CSS import order in your consumer entry CSS is `@import "tailwindcss";` then `@import "@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui/styles.css";` then your own styles тАФ the reset only wins if Lucid UI's stylesheet loads before consumer overrides.

---

#### progress-stat-card тАФ new component

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: |
    New `ProgressStatCard` component composes `Card`, `Progress`, and a tone-aware stat-cell row into a single card. Header lays out a left-side `title` with an optional right-side trailing slot тАФ `value` (bold `text-2xl`) for a single percent/number headline, OR `valueHint` (small `text-sm text-muted-foreground`) for ratio-style subtitles like `"5 of 12"`. The `Progress` bar sits below the header. The `items` prop renders 2+ stat cells underneath in a responsive grid (2-column on mobile, `auto-cols-fr grid-flow-col` row on `sm:` and above), each cell styled as `text-xl font-semibold tabular-nums` value over a `text-xs text-muted-foreground` label. Each item accepts an optional `tone` (`default | success | destructive | warning | info | muted`) that swaps the value color via the chroma tokens (`text-success`, `text-destructive`, etc.) тАФ the same pattern hand-rolled across multiple project dashboards.

    The cell tone colors come from the chroma layer (`--success`, `--destructive`, `--warning`, `--info`) and are intended as graphical emphasis on a count, treated under WCAG SC 1.4.11 (Non-text Contrast, 3:1) rather than 1.4.3. The doc page calls this out and recommends `tone="default"` plus a `Badge` or icon when AA-strict text is required.

    Closes the gap surfaced by the Pandahrms-Performance dashboard's `CycleStatusAdmin` / `CycleStatusManager` widgets, which previously hand-rolled a `<div>` card containing `Progress` plus a `<dl>` grid of stat cells. The shape is general enough to cover any "one progress reading + a breakdown of counts" surface (cycle progress, onboarding pipeline, KPI completion band).

    `Card` is composed without `CardHeader`/`CardContent` (their grid layout would force a clumsy override) тАФ the component supplies a single `p-5` padding via className on the outer Card and lays the header / progress / cells out internally. `data-slot="progress-stat-card"` is set for theming hooks. No new design tokens introduced; everything reuses existing chroma + foreground/muted-foreground + Card/Progress styles.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui` to pick up `ProgressStatCard`.
  - Replace hand-rolled `<div className="rounded-lg border bg-card p-5"><h3/><span>%</span><Progress/><dl>cells</dl></div>` cycle/pipeline cards with `<ProgressStatCard title="..." value="..." progress={n} items={[{ label, value, tone }, ...]} />`.
  - For ratio-headline variants (`5 of 12`, `12 of 18`), pass `valueHint` instead of `value`.

---

### 2026-05-02

#### button тАФ fix `asChild` crash (`React.Children.only`)

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: |
    `Button` crashed at render whenever `asChild={true}` was used. The implementation always built children as `[ResolvedIcon && <Icon/>, resolvedChildren]`, so when `Comp` was Radix `Slot` (the `asChild` branch) JSX collapsed children into a 2-item array and `Slot.SlotClone` called `React.Children.only`, throwing `React.Children.only expected to receive a single React element child`. Every `<Button asChild><a>...</a></Button>` and `<Button asChild><Link>...</Link></Button>` consumer hit this on first render.

    Fix in `packages/registry/registry/default/button/button.tsx`: split the render path. When `asChild=true`, render `<Slot>` with only `resolvedChildren` (the consumer's element). The auto-icon, the auto-tooltip wrapper, and `disabled={disabled || loading}` are only applied on the native-button path. The consumer's element owns its own internals тАФ render any icon inside the child element yourself.

    Added an "As Child (link styled as button)" section to the showcase demo (`apps/demo/src/showcase/demos/button-demo.tsx`) covering ghost / outline / default variants with `<a>` children, so a future refactor can't silently re-introduce the crash. `public/docs/button.md` now documents that `icon`, `action`, and `loading` are ignored under `asChild`.

    Variant names, prop shapes, public API, and the native-button render path are unchanged. Previously working call sites keep working; previously broken `asChild` call sites now work.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui` to pick up the fix.
  - No code changes required for consumers. `<Button asChild>` is now safe to use across all variants.
  - If you previously worked around this by applying `buttonVariants(...)` className directly to a `<Link>` / `<a>`, you can now switch to `<Button asChild>` for consistency with the rest of the registry.

#### badge / button / styles тАФ WCAG 2.1/2.2 contrast pass

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: |
    Audited Badge and Button destructive against SC 1.4.3 (Contrast Minimum, 4.5:1 for normal text тАФ Badge text is `text-xs` / 11px / semibold, classified as normal text under WCAG, NOT large text). Multiple variants failed in either or both themes; fixed by introducing scoped tokens that pass AA. The chroma tokens themselves (`--success`, `--info`, `--warning`, `--danger`, `--destructive`, `--border`) are untouched, so any component consuming `text-success` / `bg-warning` / etc. directly continues to render the same color.

    Tokens added in `packages/registry/src/styles.css` (both `:root` and `.dark`):
      - `--success-soft-fg` (light `#377336` / dark `#9fd0a2`) тАФ Badge `text-success-soft-fg` over `bg-success/15` light and `bg-success/20` dark (tints bumped from `/10` and `/15` so the chroma reads on both themes). Pre-fix contrast 3.51 / 3.24. Post-fix ~4.7 / 7.4.
      - `--info-soft-fg` (light `#2964c4` / dark `#9fc2f0`) тАФ same pattern. Pre-fix 3.50 / 3.29. Post-fix ~4.7 / 7.2.
      - `--warning-soft-fg` (light `#955b0e` / dark `#f0c47a`) тАФ same pattern. Pre-fix 2.53 / 4.33 (the worst offender). Post-fix ~5.2 / 7.2.
      - `--destructive-soft-fg` (light `#a23120` / dark `#f0a896`) тАФ reserved for future soft destructive variants; not yet wired.
      - `--destructive-aa: #a8392a` (same in both themes) тАФ solid red bg with white text. Pre-fix `#c8543f` gave 4.38:1; `#a8392a` gives 6.30:1. Used by Badge `destructive` and Button `destructive`.

    Component changes:
      - `packages/registry/registry/default/badge/badge.tsx` тАФ `success` / `warning` / `info` variants now use `text-success-soft-fg` / `text-warning-soft-fg` / `text-info-soft-fg` (was `text-success` / `text-warning` / `text-info`). `destructive` variant now uses `bg-destructive-aa` + `hover:bg-destructive-aa/90` (was `bg-destructive` + `hover:bg-destructive/80`). `muted` variant adds `dark:text-stone-300` (was `text-muted-foreground` only тАФ `stone-400` on `stone-700` measured 4.24:1; `stone-300` is ~7.3:1).
      - `packages/registry/registry/default/button/button.tsx` тАФ `destructive` variant now uses `bg-destructive-aa` (was `bg-destructive`). The `delete` action preset (`<Button action="delete">`) inherits the fix.

    Variant names, prop shapes, and component APIs are unchanged. Visual change is intentional and minor: destructive controls are a slightly darker red, and tinted-bg badges have a slightly darker (light mode) / lighter (dark mode) text color. `MeterRow`/`Alert`/`DropdownMenu` etc. that consume the chroma tokens for non-text fills or for solid `bg-destructive` are unaffected тАФ only Badge and Button were rewired to the AA tokens.

    Known gaps left intentionally for now: (1) Card boundary uses `border-border/70` (light, ~1.18:1) / `dark:border-border` (dark, ~1.47:1) тАФ under SC 1.4.11's 3:1, but the boundary is decorative and the current visual treatment was preferred over the heavier stone-400-grade border that strict compliance would require. (2) `text-destructive` used as text in `Alert` destructive, `DropdownMenu` destructive items, `Form` error messages, and `Connection-banner` still pulls the chroma `--destructive` directly. A follow-up could add a `--destructive-text` token (theme-specific) or migrate these to `text-destructive-aa`.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui`
  - No code changes required for consumers тАФ the variant names and APIs are the same. If you previously hand-replicated Badge's `bg-success/10 text-success` pattern in your own code, switch your local pattern to `text-success-soft-fg` (or define an equivalent token) so it clears AA.
  - Re-screenshot any visual snapshots/regressions covering Badge `destructive` / `success` / `warning` / `info` / `muted (dark)` and Button `destructive` тАФ colors shifted intentionally.

#### meter-row тАФ `label` is now optional; bare-bar mode skips the label row

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `MeterRow.label` is widened from required `ReactNode` to optional `ReactNode | undefined`. The component now renders the label-row container (`flex items-center justify-between gap-2 text-sm` + the outer `space-y-1` gap) only when `label != null || valueLabel !== undefined`. Previously the label row was unconditional тАФ even `label={null}` left ~24px of phantom blank space above the bar because the empty `<span>` still picked up `text-sm` line-height inside the flex container. The new behavior: pass a `label` (and optionally `valueLabel`) to keep the existing labeled layout, or omit both to render a bare progress bar that sits flush against its parent. Useful for compact list-row indicators where the count is shown separately above, KPI metric tiles where the label lives in the surrounding wrapper, and any case where the consumer renders its own label/value pair around the bar. Existing callers that pass a `label` are unchanged. Closes the gap that forced consumers to keep hand-rolled `<div className="h-X bg-muted"><div className="h-full bg-{tone}" style={{ width }} /></div>` patterns whenever they wanted a tone-aware progress bar without `MeterRow`'s built-in label slot.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui`
  - Replace remaining hand-rolled tone-aware progress bars (`<div bg-muted><div bg-success/info/warning style={{ width }}>`) with `<MeterRow value={pct} tone="success" size="sm" />` (omit `label` for bare bars). Pick `size` to match the original height: `sm`=h-1, `default`=h-1.5, `lg`=h-2.

#### package тАФ npm package renamed to `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui`

- change: renamed
- breaking: yes
- details: The npm package was renamed from `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui` so the package name matches the `lucid-ui` repo it ships from. The previous `@pandaworks-sw/ui` package on GitHub Packages stops receiving updates at v0.1.4; v0.2.0 is the first release under the new name. No source code, exports, public API, or styling tokens change тАФ only the package name and subpath import paths.
- action:
  - In `package.json`, replace `"@pandaworks-sw/ui": "<old>"` with `"@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui": "^0.2.0"`.
  - Rewrite every import: `from '@pandaworks-sw/ui'` тЖТ `from '@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui'`. Stylesheet too: `@import "@pandaworks-sw/ui/styles.css"` тЖТ `@import "@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui/styles.css"`.
  - Update Tailwind v4 source line: `@source "../node_modules/@pandaworks-sw/ui"` тЖТ `@source "../node_modules/@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui"`.
  - `.npmrc` is unchanged тАФ the scope `@pandaworks-sw` is the same; only the package within the scope changed.
  - Run `pnpm install` to refresh the lockfile.

#### multi-stat-card тАФ new component

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: New `MultiStatCard` component renders 2+ related stat metrics inside a single card with dividers between cells. Each cell mirrors `StatCard` (icon + label, animated headline value, optional hint and delta chip) and reuses the same design tokens, headline auto-shrink heuristic, and delta-chip rendering. Per-cell shape is `MultiStatCardItem` (subset of `StatCardProps` minus `className`): `icon`, `label`, `value`, `prefix`, `suffix`, `decimals`, `formatter`, `hint`, `delta`, `deltaTone`. `orientation` (default `"horizontal"`) controls divider direction (`divide-x` vs `divide-y`); cells use `flex-1 min-w-0` so widths split evenly and long values still shrink. Use it for funnel metrics (Total / In Progress / Completed), date pairs (Form Start / Submission Due), or any "metrics that belong together" cluster you don't want broken across separate tiles. Internally, the shared helpers (`DELTA_VARIANT`, `DELTA_ICON`, `getHeadlineSizeClass`, `getRenderedLength`, `DeltaTone`) were extracted from `stat-card.tsx` into a new sibling `stat-card/stat-card-shared.ts` so both components stay in sync тАФ no behavior change for existing `StatCard` usage.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - Replace ad-hoc multi-cell `Card`s (e.g. a `Card` containing a 3-column grid of label/number divs) with `<MultiStatCard items={[...]} />`
  - When mixing into a grid of single `StatCard` tiles, give the `MultiStatCard` a `col-span` equal to its cell count so the row stays balanced (e.g. `className="sm:col-span-2 xl:col-span-3"` for a 3-cell card inside an `xl:grid-cols-6` grid)

#### stat-card тАФ `value` prop now accepts strings

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `StatCard`'s `value` prop is widened from `number` to `number | string`. Numeric values keep the existing `AnimatedNumber` path (with `prefix` / `suffix` / `decimals` / `formatter` adornments). String values render as-is тАФ useful for already-formatted or non-numeric tiles such as `"N/A"`, `"тАФ"`, `"Active"`, `"Enterprise"`, `"8 / 10"`, or pre-formatted currency. `prefix`, `suffix`, `decimals`, and `formatter` are ignored when `value` is a string (strings are assumed already-formatted). The `delta` / `deltaTone` chip and `hint` continue to work for both forms.
- action:
  - No migration needed for existing numeric usage.
  - When showing a non-animated label (status, tier, ratio, "N/A"), pass the string directly instead of building a custom `Card` wrapper.

#### stat-card тАФ headline auto-shrinks for long values

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: The headline class steps down based on the rendered value's length so long values still fit inside the tile without overflowing or wrapping awkwardly: `тЙд10 chars тЖТ text-xl`, `тЙд16 тЖТ text-lg`, `тЙд24 тЖТ text-base`, `>24 тЖТ text-sm`. Length is computed from the string for string values, from `formatter(value)` when a formatter is provided, and from `prefix + integer digits + decimals + suffix` for plain numbers. Pure CSS тАФ no `ResizeObserver`, no measurement, no extra render тАФ so the cost is zero. The headline wrapper also gets `min-w-0` so it can shrink inside narrower grid columns. Short numeric values (the common case, e.g. `value={42}` or `value={73} suffix="%"`) keep `text-xl` and look identical to before.
- action:
  - No migration needed.
  - If a value is now appearing smaller than expected, count the rendered length (string, or `prefix + digits + decimals + suffix`) and compare against the thresholds. If the value is supposed to be a hero number, the tile is the wrong primitive тАФ use a plain `Card` with custom typography.

### 2026-05-01

#### avatar тАФ `getInitialName` utility + `AvatarFallback` auto-converts full names

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: New helper `getInitialName(name: string): string` exported from `@pandaworks-sw/ui` (and from the registry's internal `@/lib` alias тАФ see `packages/registry/src/lib/get-initial-name.ts`). Pass any string тАФ including a full multi-word name тАФ to `<AvatarFallback>` and it now renders a 2-character monogram automatically: `"John Doe" тЖТ "JD"`, `"Devi Marasinghe" тЖТ "DM"` (first letter of first + last word), `"Eli" тЖТ "EL"` (first 2 chars of single-word names), `""` тЖТ empty. Already-short strings such as `"JD"` or `"+3"` pass through unchanged so existing call sites that pre-compute initials keep working. Callers that previously hand-wrote a `getInitials` helper (e.g. `app-shell.tsx`) can drop it and pass `user.name` directly.
- action:
  - Optional cleanup: replace ad-hoc `getInitials(user.name)` helpers with `<AvatarFallback>{user.name}</AvatarFallback>` or `import { getInitialName } from '@pandaworks-sw/ui'` for non-Avatar contexts (aria-labels, sorting).

#### avatar тАФ `AvatarFallback` colorize default flipped to `true`

- change: modified
- breaking: yes
- details: The `colorize` prop on `AvatarFallback` now defaults to `true`. `<AvatarFallback>John Doe</AvatarFallback>` renders a deterministically colored monogram out of the box (AтАУZ map to 26 evenly spaced OKLCH hues; digits and other characters fall back to a deterministic hash; foreground is white in both light and dark mode). Pass `colorize={false}` to opt back into the muted (`bg-muted`) look тАФ useful for `+N` overflow tiles, system placeholders, and other non-name fallbacks. The `+N` overflow tile inside `AvatarGroup` (`packages/registry/registry/default/avatar-group/avatar-group.tsx`) was updated to set `colorize={false}` so it keeps its neutral background.
- action:
  - If a project relied on the previous gray default for arbitrary, non-name text content (e.g. `"+N"` chips, system tiles, single-character placeholders), add `colorize={false}` to those `AvatarFallback` instances.
  - Name-based avatars need no change тАФ they get the colorized look automatically.
  - The previous opt-in form `<AvatarFallback colorize>JD</AvatarFallback>` still works (just redundant now).

#### registry тАФ `@/lib` is now a folder alias inside the demo

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `apps/demo/vite.config.ts` replaces the file-specific `@/lib/utils` alias with a folder-level `@/lib` alias pointing at `packages/registry/src/lib/`. Existing `import { cn } from '@/lib/utils'` continues to resolve via file lookup inside the aliased folder. The new preferred form for registry source files is `import { cn, getInitialName } from '@/lib'` тАФ backed by a new `packages/registry/src/lib/index.ts` barrel.
- action: nothing required for downstream consumers тАФ they import from `@pandaworks-sw/ui`, not from `@/lib`. The change only affects internal registry source files.

#### app-shell тАФ handle small screens (mobile view)

- change: fixed
- breaking: no
- details: Three mobile/compact-viewport UX bugs in `packages/registry/registry/default/app-shell/app-shell.tsx`. (1) The mobile Sheet sidebar did not auto-close when the user tapped a nav link, the branding link, or a user-action link тАФ the route changed but the Sheet stayed mounted, forcing manual dismissal. Each link now calls `setOpenMobile(false)` on click via a small `useMobileAutoClose` hook (no-op on desktop). (2) The header content area lacked `min-w-0` / `shrink-0` on its flex children, so a wide consumer `header` (e.g. a search input with long placeholder) wrapped or pushed `navbarActions` off-screen at narrow widths instead of shrinking. The header wrapper, the header content slot, the sidebar trigger, the separator, and the navbar-actions slot now have explicit `min-w-0` / `shrink-0` so consumer content shrinks gracefully. (3) The full 18rem sidebar squeezed the navbar at compact desktop widths (768тАУ1023px) тАФ for example, the saas demo's search input was truncated to "SearcтАж" at 768px. AppShell now controls `SidebarProvider`'s `open` state via the new `useIsCompactDesktop` hook (`packages/registry/src/hooks/use-mobile.ts`); the sidebar collapses to icon mode automatically inside the compact range and expands past 1024px. Mobile (<768px) keeps using the Sheet variant.
- action: nothing required for consumers. Existing `linkComponent` integrations keep working because the auto-close handler is attached as `onClick` and is forwarded through the consumer's link via spread. Apps that pass a wide custom `header` should still apply their own internal `truncate` / `min-w-0` to long text тАФ AppShell now allows the slot to shrink, but the consumer is responsible for what happens inside it. The compact-viewport auto-collapse is automatic тАФ AppShell no longer accepts a `defaultOpen` prop, but consumers who need controlled-open behavior can still wrap their own `SidebarProvider`. The new `useIsCompactDesktop` hook is also re-exported via the app-shell barrel for consumers wanting matching responsive logic in their own headers.

#### design-token stylesheet now ships with the package

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: The package now exposes `@pandaworks-sw/ui/styles.css` as a subpath export. The file contains the full design system the components rely on тАФ `:root` / `.dark` token blocks, the `@theme inline` mapping that produces Tailwind v4 utilities (`bg-primary`, `text-brand`, `bg-stone-200`, `text-panda-500`, etc.), the base layer (border-color reset, fonts on `html` / headings / `code`), the `text-display-{sm,md,lg,xl}` and `text-mono-{sm,md}` typography utilities, and the `bg-pattern-*` background utilities (dots, grid, cross, noise, aurora + 9 aurora colour variants). Source of truth: `packages/registry/src/styles.css`. Build pipeline: `tsup` `onSuccess` hook copies the file into `dist/styles.css` on every build; `package.json` `exports` adds `"./styles.css": "./dist/styles.css"`. The demo's `apps/demo/src/index.css` was reduced to four lines and now imports the same file directly so demo and package can never drift.
- action:
  - Replace the hand-copied token CSS in your app's entry stylesheet with:
    ```css
    @import "tailwindcss";
    @import "@pandaworks-sw/ui/styles.css";
    @source "../node_modules/@pandaworks-sw/ui";
    ```
  - The `@source` line is required so Tailwind v4 scans the package's compiled JSX for class names тАФ without it, utilities used inside `@pandaworks-sw/ui` components will be tree-shaken out of your CSS bundle.
  - Fonts are not bundled: keep loading Inter, Comfortaa, and JetBrains Mono yourself (Google Fonts `<link>` in `index.html` is fine; see `apps/demo/index.html`).

#### unused build output, scripts, and configs removed

- change: removed
- breaking: yes
- details: With the npm package on GitHub Packages now the only distribution channel, the project drops the legacy build artefacts that produced a separate JSON-based component output. Files deleted: `public/r/` (61 component JSONs), `packages/registry/registry.json`, the per-workspace `components.json` files, `packages/registry/scripts/rewrite-lucid-namespace.mjs`, `packages/registry/scripts/sync-all-meta.mjs`, `packages/registry/registry/default/_all/`, and `apps/demo/src/components/install-command.tsx`. Configs simplified: root `package.json` drops the `registry:build` script and reworks `build` to run `@pandaworks-sw/ui build:lib && @pandaworks-sw/demo build`; `packages/registry/package.json` drops `registry:build`, simplifies `build` to `tsup`, and removes the `shadcn` devDep; `biome.json` drops the `!public/r` ignore entries; `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` no longer copies `public/r` into the Pages artifact; `apps/demo/src/showcase/component-page.tsx` and `showcase-app.tsx` drop the `<InstallCommand />` widget and its `installName` prop. Documentation in `CLAUDE.md` and `README.md` updated accordingly.
- action:
  - For new projects: install with `pnpm add @pandaworks-sw/ui` after configuring the `.npmrc` for GitHub Packages (see Installation above).
  - For existing apps still vendoring `src/components/lucid/`: migrate by adding `@pandaworks-sw/ui` as a dependency, running a codemod to rewrite `from '@/components/lucid/<name>'` тЖТ `from '@pandaworks-sw/ui'`, and deleting the `src/components/lucid/` folder.

#### npm package scope rename + GitHub Packages distribution

- change: renamed
- breaking: yes
- details: The npm package was renamed from `@lucid/ui` to `@pandaworks-sw/ui` and is now published to **GitHub Packages** (not the public npm registry). The demo workspace was likewise renamed from `@lucid/demo` to `@pandaworks-sw/demo`. GitHub Packages requires the npm scope to match the GitHub org owning the repository, so the repo is moving to `github.com/pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui`. A new release workflow at `.github/workflows/publish.yml` publishes the package whenever a GitHub Release is published.
- action:
  - Add an `.npmrc` at the project root with `@pandaworks-sw:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com` and `//npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken=${GITHUB_TOKEN}`. Set `GITHUB_TOKEN` to a classic PAT with `read:packages` scope on every developer machine and CI job. Update `package.json` from `@lucid/ui` to `@pandaworks-sw/ui`, then rewrite all `from '@lucid/ui'` imports to `from '@pandaworks-sw/ui'`. Run `pnpm install` to refresh the lockfile.

#### rebrand (Pandaworks UI тЖТ Lucid UI)

- change: renamed
- breaking: yes
- details: Library rebranded from `Pandaworks UI` to `Lucid UI`. The npm package was renamed from `@pandaworks-ui/components` to `@lucid/ui`. The demo workspace was renamed from `@pandaworks-ui/demo` to `@lucid/demo`. The GitHub repo was renamed from `pandaworks-software-plt/pandaworks-ui` to `pandaworks-software-plt/lucid-ui`; GitHub auto-redirects the old URL. Internal-only library тАФ no external consumers were affected.
- action:
  - Update `package.json` from `@pandaworks-ui/components` to `@lucid/ui`, then update all `from '@pandaworks-ui/components'` imports to `from '@lucid/ui'`

#### npm-package distribution

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: Component sources now publish as the `@pandaworks-ui/components` npm package. The previously private `packages/registry/` workspace package was renamed from `@pandaworks-ui/registry` to `@pandaworks-ui/components`, made public, and given a proper `exports` map (`./dist/index.js` ESM + `./dist/index.d.ts`). New build artifacts: [packages/registry/src/index.ts](packages/registry/src/index.ts) re-exports all 59 component folders + hooks (`useCopyToClipboard`, plus `useIsMobile` via the `app-shell` barrel) + the `cn` util; [packages/registry/tsup.config.ts](packages/registry/tsup.config.ts) emits ESM + DTS to `packages/registry/dist/` (185 KB ESM, 56 KB types, sourcemaps, tree-shakeable with `sideEffects: false`). Peer deps: `react >=19`, `react-dom >=19`, `lucide-react >=0.500`, `react-hook-form >=7`, `tailwindcss >=4`.
- action:
  - For new Pandahrms apps: `pnpm add @pandaworks-ui/components` and `import { Button, Badge, тАж } from '@pandaworks-ui/components'`
  - Tailwind v4 must already be configured in the consumer with the design tokens this library expects (`--primary`, `--brand`, `--success`, `--info`, `--warning`, `--muted`, etc.). See `apps/demo/src/index.css` for the canonical token set

#### empty-state, list-row (TypeScript fixes)

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `EmptyState` and `ListRow` both define a `title: ReactNode` prop that previously conflicted with the inherited HTML `title?: string` attribute. `EmptyState` now uses `Omit<HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "title">` on its base type. `ListRow`'s internal spread casts (`rest as ButtonProps` / `rest as DivProps`) now use `Omit<тАж, keyof SharedListRowProps>` so the rest object no longer re-introduces `title` after destructuring. No public API change. Existing usage continues to compile and render the same. The fix unblocks the demo build (and the GitHub Pages deploy job), which was failing with `TS2430` / `TS2322`.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up the typed-correctly versions

#### split-button (new brand variant)

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `SplitButton` gains a `brand` variant matching `Button`'s brand styling (`bg-brand text-brand-foreground shadow-xs hover:shadow-sm`) plus a corresponding entry in `dividerClasses` (`border-l border-brand-foreground/20`). Closes a parity gap surfaced by the pure-showcase Projects page, which already used `<SplitButton variant="brand">` for its "New project" CTA but compiled-failed because the variant didn't exist. Existing variants (`default`, `destructive`, `outline`, `secondary`, `ghost`) are unchanged.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up the brand variant
  - Use `<SplitButton variant="brand">` for primary "create"-style split actions; pairs visually with `<Button action="create">`

#### stat-card

- change: modified
- breaking: yes (visual)
- details: `StatCard` is now a single SaaS-density size тАФ tight padding (`px-4`), `text-xs` label, `size-3.5` icon, and a `text-xl` headline number. The previous `text-3xl` headline ate too much screen estate inside dashboards. No API change: the `size` prop introduced earlier today was removed before any consumer adopted it. Existing `<StatCard тАж />` usage continues to compile but renders smaller; tighten any surrounding grid gaps (`gap-4` тЖТ `gap-3`) if the layout looks too sparse.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up the new sizing
  - Audit pages that use `StatCard` тАФ the visual is smaller, so previously cramped dashboards will breathe but very sparse layouts may need extra tiles or tighter grid gaps

#### attachment-input, button, split-button, pagination, breadcrumb

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: Five components now set `position: relative` on the root that wraps an `sr-only` accessibility node. Tailwind's `sr-only` utility uses `position: absolute`; without a positioned ancestor it falls through to the initial containing block (the viewport) and lays out at its static offset, stretching the document height. Pages that rendered several of these components (the showcase being the worst case) could scroll past the bottom into empty space. Affected roots: `AttachmentInput` wrapper, `buttonVariants` (Button) base, `splitButtonVariants` (SplitButton) base, `PaginationEllipsis`, `BreadcrumbEllipsis`. The `Button` change also fixes consumers like `FilterButton` and `SidebarTrigger` that nest `<span className="sr-only">тАж</span>` inside a `<Button>`.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up the fix
  - No prop or API changes; existing usage works as-is

#### avatar

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `AvatarFallback` gains an optional `colorize` boolean prop. When passed, the background color is derived from the first character of the fallback text тАФ AтАУZ map to 26 evenly spaced OKLCH hues (`oklch(0.62 0.14 hue)`), digits and other characters fall back to a deterministic hash. Foreground is set to white. The default `bg-muted` fallback styling is unchanged when `colorize` is not set.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up the new prop
  - Opt in per usage: `<AvatarFallback colorize>JD</AvatarFallback>`
  - Custom `className` / `style` still take precedence over the computed colors

#### table

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `Table` wrapper drops its own border, rounded corners, `bg-card`, and shadow when nested inside `CardContent`, so the surrounding card frame is the only visual boundary (fixes "card within a card" appearance). Standalone `Table` usage is unchanged.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up the nesting-aware wrapper. `CardContent` now sets `data-slot="card-content"`, which is required for the table to detect the card context

#### card

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `CardContent` now sets `data-slot="card-content"` on its root element so descendant components (e.g. `Table`) can adapt their styling when nested inside.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - No code changes required in consumers

#### badge

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `Badge` gains four semantic-tone variants тАФ `success` / `warning` / `info` / `muted` тАФ keyed off the existing `--success` / `--info` / `--warning` / `--muted` design tokens, and a new `dot` boolean prop that renders a leading status dot tinted to match the variant. Default visual for the original four variants (`default`, `secondary`, `destructive`, `outline`) is unchanged. Closes registry gap G1.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up the new variants
  - Replace bespoke status pills (`<span className="bg-emerald-50 text-emerald-700 тАж">`) with `<Badge variant="success" dot>Active</Badge>`

#### avatar

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `Avatar` accepts a new `shape` prop (`"circle"` (default) or `"square"`). Square avatars use `rounded-md` and inherit through `AvatarFallback` so the fallback layer stays in sync (e.g. while an image loads). Useful for project tiles, app icons, and other entity avatars where round + person doesn't fit. Closes registry gap G2. The previously shipped `colorize` prop on `AvatarFallback` is unchanged.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - Existing usage is unchanged; opt in via `<Avatar shape="square">`

#### avatar-group

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: New `AvatarGroup` component that horizontally stacks `Avatar` children with negative spacing (`-space-x-1` / `-space-x-2` / `-space-x-3`), an outline ring against the page background, and a `+N` overflow tile when more avatars are passed than the `max` prop. Inherits the per-Avatar `shape` (or accepts a group-level `shape` prop). Sizes: `xs` / `sm` / `md` / `lg`. Closes registry gap G3.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - Wrap your existing avatar stacks: `<AvatarGroup max={3} size="md">{members.map(тАж)}</AvatarGroup>`

#### empty-state

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: New `EmptyState` component for filtered tables/lists, "no results" panels, and any centered "nothing to show" surface. Slots for `icon`, `title`, `description`, and an `action`. Three sizes (`sm` / `md` / `lg`) controlling vertical padding and icon-tile size. Wraps the icon in a soft muted disc. Closes registry gap G4.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - Replace hand-rolled `<div className="flex flex-col items-center gap-3 py-16 тАж">` empty blocks with `<EmptyState icon={тАж} title="тАж" action={тАж} />`

#### stat-card

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: New `StatCard` component for dashboard tiles. Composes registry primitives (`Card`, `AnimatedNumber`, `Badge`) and accepts: `icon`, `label`, `value`, `suffix`, `hint`, `delta`, `deltaTone` (`"up"` / `"down"` / `"flat"`). The delta chip resolves to a Badge `success` / `destructive` / `muted` variant with the appropriate trend icon (`TrendingUp` / `TrendingDown` / `Minus`). Closes registry gap G5.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - Existing dashboard stat cards collapse from ~30 lines of Card + custom delta chip to a single `<StatCard тАж>`

#### dropdown-menu

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `DropdownMenuItem` accepts a new `variant` prop (`"default"` (default) or `"destructive"`). When set to `"destructive"`, the item text and any nested icon use `text-destructive`, and the focus state shifts to a tinted destructive background (`focus:bg-destructive/10`). Replaces the manual `className="text-destructive focus:text-destructive"` pattern. Closes registry gap G9.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - Replace `<DropdownMenuItem className="text-destructive focus:text-destructive">` with `<DropdownMenuItem variant="destructive">`

#### code-label

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `CodeLabel` accepts a new `copyable` boolean prop (default `true`). Pass `copyable={false}` for a static inline code chip with no copy affordance тАФ useful when the label is purely visual (e.g. listing many project keys in a budget summary table). Existing usage is unchanged. Closes registry gap G8.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - Existing usage is unchanged; opt in via `<CodeLabel value={key} copyable={false} />`

#### meter-row

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: New `MeterRow` component тАФ labeled horizontal bar with optional trailing value and per-row tone (`default`, `success`, `warning`, `info`, `destructive`, `muted`). Sizes (`sm` / `default` / `lg`). Replaces the hand-rolled `<div className="h-1.5 w-full overflow-hidden rounded-full bg-muted"><div style={{ width: \`${pct}%\` }} />` pattern that was repeated across `Dashboard.DepartmentChart` and the `Reports` status / priority charts. Closes registry gap G6.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - Replace hand-rolled labeled bars with `<MeterRow label={тАж} value={n} max={total} valueLabel={n} tone="success" />`

#### list-row

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: New `ListRow` component тАФ `leading + title + subtitle + trailing` layout primitive used across the SaaS demo's activity feeds, deadline lists, team lists, and API key lists. Pass `asButton` to render as a keyboard-accessible button with focus ring; otherwise renders as a div. Density variants (`sm` / `default` / `lg`) and surface variants (`plain` / `bordered` / `muted`). Closes registry gap G7.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - Collapse manual `<div className="flex items-center gap-3 тАж">` row layouts into `<ListRow leading={тАж} title={тАж} subtitle={тАж} trailing={тАж} />`

#### decision-guide

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: New `public/docs/decision-guide.md` is the canonical "which variant / tone / surface / size?" reference. Covers Avatar shape (human тЖТ `circle`, non-human entity тЖТ `square`), Badge tones with the `dot` rule, Alert variants, MeterRow tones, ListRow surface, Tabs `line` vs pill, StatCard `deltaTone` (tone follows desirability, not arrow direction), Modal vs Dialog vs Sheet vs AlertDialog, text-input choice (Input / Textarea / SearchInput / CodeLabel), dropdown choice (Select for static < 10 / SelectPicker otherwise), date controls, boolean / multi-choice rules (Switch = immediate effect; Checkbox = collected on Save), AttachmentInput dropzone vs compact, dashboard tile choice (StatCard / MeterRow / ListRow / Card), the registry's fixed elevation ladder (`shadow-none` тЖТ `shadow-xs/sm` тЖТ `shadow-md` тЖТ `shadow-xl`) with an explicit note that `Card` has no `elevation` prop and is fixed at `shadow-xs` by design, DropdownMenuItem `default` vs `destructive`, and labelled vs icon-only Button rules. The Decision Guide section in `public/llms.txt` now links to it.
- action: no consumer action тАФ read this when picking a variant/tone/surface

### 2026-04-23

#### tooltip

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `TooltipContent` now wraps Radix `Portal` so the popper layer mounts on `document.body`, avoiding clipping and stacking issues inside scrollable regions or transformed layout (same pattern as `PopoverContent`).
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up the change
  - No API changes; custom `className` / props on `TooltipContent` behave as before

#### stepper

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: Smaller circles (`size-7`) and labels; completed steps and finished track segments use emerald (`emerald-600` / `emerald-500` in dark). Current step uses primary border plus a light ring. New optional `orientation` prop: `"vertical"` renders a top-down list with a left spine. Horizontal layout keeps leading/trailing `flex-1` segments for even spacing. Step `description` and optional `tooltip` popover render only when that step is the current index. Track segments layer a muted base with an emerald fill that animates with `scale-x` / `scale-y` (~500ms, `ease-out`); `prefers-reduced-motion` removes the transition.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up visuals and `orientation`
  - Override `className` if you need larger step markers than the new defaults

#### code-label

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `CodeLabel` now uses a subtler filled background (no outline border). The copy button is hidden by default and reveals with width/opacity expansion on hover/focus, appears on touch/press for mobile, and stays visible briefly after touch release and copy success feedback.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to get the updated behavior and styling
  - No API changes; existing `value`, `size`, and `className` usage stays the same

#### sheet

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: Sheet panels are inset from the viewport (floating card), use rounded corners (`rounded-2xl`), `shadow-xl`, a light ring, and `overflow-y-auto` with overscroll containment. No enter/exit animation classes on overlay or content (Radix still handles mount/unmount timing).
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up styles
  - If you relied on edge-to-edge sheets, override `className` on `SheetContent` to restore full-bleed layout

#### button

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `brand`, `destructive`, `outline`, and `secondary` variants now use `shadow-xs` with `hover:shadow-sm` instead of bespoke multi-layer inset/box shadows. Visual weight is slightly flatter.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` if you want the updated styling
  - No prop or API changes

#### dialog

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `DialogOverlay` uses `bg-black/50` with `backdrop-blur-[1px]`. `DialogContent` adds `border-border` and uses `shadow-md` instead of `shadow-lg`.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`

#### command

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: Root `Command` panel adds `border border-border/70` and `shadow-md`. `CommandGroup` heading styles use Tailwind v4 `**:[[cmdk-group-heading]]:тАж` descendant variants instead of `[&_[cmdk-group-heading]]:тАж`.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - If you overrode group heading styles via global CSS on `[cmdk-group-heading]`, re-check specificity against the new utilities

#### dropdown-menu

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: Content/sub-content use `border-border`, `min-w-32`, and `shadow-md` on sub-content. Items use `data-disabled:` variant selectors instead of `data-[disabled]:`. `max-h` uses `max-h-(--radix-dropdown-menu-content-available-height)`.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`

#### select

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `SelectTrigger` uses `shadow-xs` / `focus:shadow-sm` and `data-placeholder:text-muted-foreground`. `SelectContent` uses `min-w-32`, `shadow-sm`, and `data-disabled:` on items. Popper viewport uses `h-(--radix-select-trigger-height)` and `min-w-(--radix-select-trigger-width)`.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`

#### popover

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `PopoverContent` adds explicit `border-border` alongside existing border and shadow utilities.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`

#### app-shell

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `AppShell` now uses the inset sidebar variant by default, and the inset main surface uses a larger rounded frame (`rounded-3xl`) with existing border/shadow layering for a blended dashboard shell look.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up the updated shell framing
  - No API changes; existing `AppShellProps` usage remains compatible

#### registry batch (alert-dialog, app-shell, attachment-input, card, input, modal, selectable-card, split-button, table, textarea)

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: Minor className alignment for Tailwind v4 patterns and visual consistency (e.g. `border-border`, shadow tokens, `data-disabled` where Radix exposes the attribute, small layout tokens). No intentional public API changes.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up pixel-identical styling

### 2026-04-08

#### detail-page

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: Added `DetailPageSidebarSection` sub-component. A sidebar section with header row (label + optional action slot) and free-form children. Inspired by GitHub issue detail sidebar pattern.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - No code changes required. Existing `DetailPageMetaItem` usage is unaffected.
  - New `DetailPageSidebarSection` is available for use alongside or instead of `DetailPageMetaItem`

#### repo rename

- change: renamed
- breaking: yes
- details: Repository renamed from `pandahrms-ui-registry` to `pandaworks-ui`.
- action:
  - Update any references to the old repo URL.

#### button

- change: modified
- breaking: yes
- details: `default` variant is now standard shadcn/ui style (plain). Previous `default` (blue with inset shadows) moved to new `brand` variant.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up the new defaults
  - Search codebase for `variant="default"` on `<Button>` -- if the intent was the blue styled button, change to `variant="brand"`
  - Buttons with no explicit `variant` prop now render as the plain default -- verify this is acceptable or add `variant="brand"` where the blue button was expected
  - Action presets `create` and `save` already updated to use `brand` -- no change needed if using `action="create"` or `action="save"`

---

### 2026-04-03

#### sonner

- change: modified
- breaking: yes
- details: Decoupled from `next-themes`. No longer imports or depends on ThemeProvider. Improved type safety and accessibility.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - If your project was relying on sonner auto-detecting theme from `next-themes`, you may need to pass the `theme` prop explicitly

#### all components (24 affected)

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: Added `data-slot` attributes for DOM identification. Standardized double quotes. No API changes.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up the changes
  - No code changes required in consuming projects

#### barrel exports (19 components)

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: Added `index.ts` barrel exports for avatar, badge, button, calendar, card, checkbox, collapsible, command, dialog, dropdown-menu, input, label, popover, separator, sheet, sidebar, toggle, tooltip, app-shell-types.
- action:
  - No action required. Consuming projects can optionally update imports to use barrel exports

#### useCopyToClipboard

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: New hook in `src/hooks/use-copy-to-clipboard.ts`. Copies text to clipboard with a reset timer.
- action:
  - Available for use in consuming projects. No action required unless you want to use it.

---

### 2026-03-31

#### button

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: Added `action` prop with 12 presets (create, edit, save, delete, cancel, view, export, import, archive, duplicate, print, link). Added `icon`, `tooltip`, and `loading` props. Auto-resolves icon, variant, label, and tooltip from action presets.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - Replace all `<CreateButton>`, `<EditButton>`, `<SaveButton>`, etc. with `<Button action="create">`, `<Button action="edit">`, `<Button action="save">`, etc.
  - Replace `<ConfirmableActionButton>` with `<AlertDialog>` wrapping `<Button action="delete">`
  - Remove all imports from `@/components/ui/action-button/*`

#### action-button

- change: removed
- breaking: yes
- details: Entire component removed (15 files). All functionality consolidated into Button `action` prop.
- action:
  - Delete `@/components/ui/action-button/` directory from consuming projects
  - See button entry above for migration steps

#### alert-dialog

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: Internal refactor -- AlertDialog action/cancel buttons now use `<Button>` via `asChild` instead of applying `buttonVariants` classes directly.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - No code changes required in consuming projects
